source_pr.title: store/tikv/latch: fix a deadlock in the latch scheduler
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

Fix a deadlock in the latch scheduler.
If a transaction release it's lock, it should awake other transactions.
But there is a bug that the transaction does not do that, so some transactions will never get the signal and wait forever.
The blocked transactions will occupy memory and may lead to OOM eventually.

### What is changed and how it works?

When a key release its lock, let the next lock take the key automatically.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Should cherry pick to release-2.1

@disksing @zhangjinpeng1987

symptoms: Fix a deadlock in the latch scheduler (store/tikv/latch/scheduler.go)
failure_signature: Fix a deadlock in the latch scheduler (store/tikv/latch/scheduler.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test/system could hang intermittently due to a timing-dependent concurrency bug: the latch scheduler could deadlock when a transaction released a lock but did not reliably wake up the next waiter. This PR fixes the scheduler to hand off the key to the next lock on release, preventing wait-forever behavior.
fix_pattern: Fix concurrency deadlock by ensuring waiters are awakened/handed off when releasing latches.

evidence: evidence: Fix a deadlock in the latch scheduler (store/tikv/latch/scheduler.go)
